title: Installer, Localhost Smoke, Uninstall, Clean, And Performance Capture Tasklist
mainGoal: Run one reproducible Linux lifecycle test that covers installer or setup execution, localhost scaffold access, safe shutdown, uninstall, explicit data cleanup, and a performance report for each major step in the sequence.

tasklistPolicy.orderingRule: Ordered by one real operator lifecycle: prepare a disposable target, install or set up the system, validate localhost traffic, stop it cleanly, remove it, purge preserved data, then publish the measured results.
tasklistPolicy.focusRule: Use the real project-facing scripts and smoke routes instead of handcrafted shortcuts. Favor decisive pass/fail checks plus timing capture over broad exploratory testing.
tasklistPolicy.readerNote: This is a focused execution tasklist for an end-to-end installer and cleanup cycle. It is narrower than the broader system-improvement backlog and is intended to produce one auditable operational run plus one matching performance report.

sourceReports.topic: Baseline measurement plan for boot, request, spawn, restart, and shutdown timing
HTTP smoke-path behavior, request progression, and route-level validation scope
Boot, supervision, shutdown, and child-lifecycle correctness
Scaffold tenant creation, localhost host alias behavior, and tenant route expectations

taskGroups.title: Prepare A Disposable Installer Lifecycle Target
taskGroups.goal: Make the end-to-end run reproducible and safe to execute without accidentally deleting the authoring workspace or mixing in residue from an older install.
pointsOfAttention: The full install-to-uninstall cycle should run in a disposable Linux target or disposable checkout.
If `installer.sh` is launched from a checkout that already contains `app/` and `setup/`, it reuses that checkout; later uninstall would remove that same project directory.
Keep one separate checkout available for `setup/purge-data.sh`, because uninstall intentionally removes the installed project while preserving data directories.

tasks.title: Choose the disposable execution shape
tasks.reportedGoal: Ensure the lifecycle run can cover install, uninstall, and purge without risking the working repository.

subtasks.title: Use a fresh machine, VM, container, or disposable clone for the actual lifecycle run
Prefer the true `installer.sh` clone flow when validating the full install path, so the installed project lives under `/opt/ehecoatl` instead of the authoring checkout
Keep a second checkout available to run `setup/purge-data.sh` after uninstall removes the installed project tree

tasks.title: Define timing checkpoints and pre-run state checks
tasks.reportedGoal: Start the run with clear measurement boundaries and a known-clean operational baseline.

subtasks.title: Record OS, kernel, Node.js, Redis, PM2, and whether package caches are warm or cold
Capture absolute timestamps for installer or setup start, setup prompt answer, runtime start, first successful localhost request, stop, uninstall, and purge
Confirm no old `Ehecoatl` PM2 process, stale `/opt/ehecoatl`, or leftover `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` data will contaminate the run

taskGroups.title: Run Installer Or Setup With A Localhost Scaffold
taskGroups.goal: Execute the real installation path and finish with a scaffold tenant that is reachable through the localhost host alias.
pointsOfAttention: Use `installer.sh` when validating the fresh-install story; use `setup/setup.sh` only when the goal is specifically the existing-checkout path.
Answer `localhost` at the scaffold prompt so setup creates `www.localhost`, which the tenancy adapter also serves through `localhost`.
Record any manual intervention, retry, or prerequisite issue because those are part of the installer-quality outcome.

tasks.title: Execute the operator-facing install path
tasks.reportedGoal: Complete one real install or setup run without bypassing the published scripts.

subtasks.title: Run `./installer.sh` for the fresh-install validation path, or `./setup/setup.sh` when intentionally validating the existing-checkout setup path
At the optional tenant prompt, enter `localhost` so setup scaffolds the default `tenant-minimal` host as `www.localhost`
Verify install metadata, runtime directories, CLI symlink, and tenant files were created in the expected locations

tasks.title: Capture install and setup timings
tasks.reportedGoal: Turn the install phase into measurable data instead of a simple pass/fail claim.

subtasks.title: Measure total installer or setup wall-clock duration
Record notable subphase durations when visible, such as dependency installation, `npm install`, and the first-tenant scaffold step
Note whether the run was fully unattended after launch or required manual fixes, reruns, or extra privileges

taskGroups.title: Validate Localhost Smoke Routes And Safe Stop
taskGroups.goal: Prove the scaffold tenant is reachable through localhost, the expected routes answer correctly, and the runtime shuts down without supervision drift.
pointsOfAttention: Use the current smoke route set from the bundled `tenant-minimal` template.
The localhost proof should demonstrate both host resolution correctness and actual request-path correctness.
Capture both first-hit and repeated-route timings where practical, because tenant spawn and warm-path costs are different.

tasks.title: Boot the runtime and verify localhost access
tasks.reportedGoal: Confirm the installed runtime can serve the scaffold tenant locally through the real boot path.

subtasks.title: Start the runtime with `cd app && sudo npm run start`
Send local requests through the active listener using `localhost` host resolution, for example `http://localhost` or `http://127.0.0.1` with `Host: localhost` when needed
Verify `GET /`, `GET /htm/index.htm`, `GET /htm/cached.htm`, `GET /hello`, and `GET /session` against the scaffold tenant

tasks.title: Validate response semantics and request-path stability
tasks.reportedGoal: Confirm the localhost smoke checks prove more than mere port reachability.

subtasks.title: Confirm `/` returns a redirect to `/htm/index.htm`
Confirm `/htm/index.htm` and `/htm/cached.htm` return the expected HTML bodies
Confirm `/hello` returns JSON containing `message` and `timestampUtc`, and `/session` returns JSON containing `message`, `sessionId`, and `timestampUtc` without a generic 500 or 502

tasks.title: Measure runtime start, route latency, and shutdown
tasks.reportedGoal: Capture the performance profile of the localhost smoke run while also proving the stop path stays clean.

subtasks.title: Measure cold start wall-clock time to the first successful localhost response
Capture repeated timings for `/htm/index.htm`, `/hello`, and `/session`, with `/htm/cached.htm` included when cache behavior is part of the run
Stop the runtime with `cd app && sudo npm run stop`, confirm children exit cleanly, and record shutdown duration

taskGroups.title: Uninstall And Explicitly Clean Preserved Data
taskGroups.goal: Prove the documented uninstall contract is correct: binaries and project files are removable first, and preserved runtime data can be destroyed only through an explicit purge step.
pointsOfAttention: Uninstall and purge are separate by design; do not collapse them into one undocumented shortcut.
Verify the preservation contract after uninstall before running purge.
Because uninstall removes the installed project tree, the purge step should be launched from another checkout or from a preserved copy of the repository.

tasks.title: Run uninstall and verify the preservation boundary
tasks.reportedGoal: Confirm uninstall removes the installed project artifacts while leaving custom runtime data intact.

subtasks.title: Execute `./setup/uninstall.sh` from the installed project
Verify the CLI symlink, installed project files, and install metadata are removed
Verify `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` remain present until an explicit purge is run

tasks.title: Run the destructive purge and confirm the environment is clean
tasks.reportedGoal: Finish the lifecycle run with a truly clean post-test state.

subtasks.title: From another checkout, execute `./setup/purge-data.sh` and provide the required `PURGE` confirmation
Verify `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` are removed after purge
Confirm no residual local tenant data, PM2 process entry, or install metadata remains from the tested run

tasks.title: Capture uninstall and purge timings
tasks.reportedGoal: Record removal cost and any cleanup anomalies with the same rigor used for install and runtime timings.

subtasks.title: Measure uninstall wall-clock duration
Measure purge wall-clock duration
Record any manual cleanup or unexpected residue that the scripts did not handle

taskGroups.title: Publish The Lifecycle Performance Report
taskGroups.goal: Turn the installer-to-cleanup run into one concrete performance artifact that shows pass or fail status plus timings for each phase.
pointsOfAttention: The report should capture operational truth, not only success claims.
Include command shapes, environment details, cold versus warm distinctions, and any manual intervention.
If any phase fails, the report should name the exact phase, symptom, and next blocking task.

tasks.title: Write the end-to-end performance report
tasks.reportedGoal: Create one `report/performance` artifact that covers the full installer, localhost, shutdown, uninstall, and purge cycle.

subtasks.title: Record environment details, command shapes, and absolute run timestamps
Include timings for install or setup, first successful localhost response, repeated route checks, stop, uninstall, and purge
Summarize pass or fail outcomes, anomalies, retries, and any gaps that prevented a trustworthy baseline

tasks.title: Convert the result into the next action
tasks.reportedGoal: Make the lifecycle run actionable whether it passes cleanly or exposes a blocker.

subtasks.title: If all phases pass, mark the installer-localhost-uninstall lifecycle as validated for the tested revision
If any phase fails, write a follow-up tasklist or report entry that names the failed phase, observed symptom, and recommended next fix

executionGateForInstallerLocalhostLifecy.description: This lifecycle run is complete only when one disposable Linux target can be installed or set up, serve the scaffold tenant locally through localhost, stop cleanly, uninstall cleanly, purge preserved data explicitly, and produce a timing-backed performance report for the full sequence.
requiredChecks: A disposable execution target is chosen so uninstall does not destroy the authoring workspace
Installer or setup completes and scaffolds the `tenant-minimal` host for `www.localhost`
Runtime stop completes without hanging or supervised-child teardown drift
Uninstall removes binaries, project files, and install metadata while preserving runtime data
Purge removes the preserved `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` trees
A `report/performance` artifact is written for install, smoke, stop, uninstall, and purge timings
